context: Tests whether the agent uses win32com.client.constants named constants for all Excel enum values rather than hard-coding bare integers. An agent without the skill may write literal values like -4108 for xlCenter or 51 for xlOpenXMLWorkbook, which may differ across Excel versions and are error-prone.

checklist.name: xlCenter via constants
checklist.description: Code uses a named constant for horizontal center alignment, e.g. c.xlCenter or win32com.client.constants.xlCenter — not the bare integer -4108

checklist.name: No bare -4108 for alignment
checklist.description: Code does NOT contain the integer literal -4108 as the alignment value

checklist.name: Border line style via constants
checklist.description: Code uses a named constant for the border line style, e.g. c.xlContinuous or win32com.client.constants.xlContinuous — not a bare integer

checklist.name: No bare integer for xlContinuous
checklist.description: Code does NOT use the bare integer 1 as the LineStyle value for the continuous border (or any other unlabelled integer for border style)

checklist.name: xlOpenXMLWorkbook via constants
checklist.description: Code uses a named constant for the xlsx file format in SaveAs, e.g. c.xlOpenXMLWorkbook or win32com.client.constants.xlOpenXMLWorkbook — not a bare integer

checklist.name: No bare 51 for file format
checklist.description: Code does NOT contain the integer literal 51 as the FileFormat argument to SaveAs

checklist.name: Border weight via constants
checklist.description: Code uses a named constant for the border weight (e.g. c.xlThin, c.xlMedium) rather than a bare integer

checklist.name: win32com.client imported
checklist.description: Code imports win32com.client at the top of the script

checklist.name: Constants accessor created
checklist.description: Code creates a named reference to the constants object, e.g. c = win32com.client.constants, for use throughout the script

checklist.name: No other bare xl enum integers
checklist.description: Code does not use other bare negative integers as Excel enum values (e.g., -4108, -4107, -4131 etc.) anywhere in the script
